turns.content: Hey, I'm trying to port an existing Rust project to a new language and am a bit stuck on the performance front. I've read about flamegraphs with cargo-flamegraph, but I still don't quite get how it works or when to use it.

Yeah, that's totally normal — profiling can be tricky! So, first of all, what are you trying to optimize? Are you seeing slow parts in your existing Rust project, or is this more about understanding where the new language spends its time?
I'm noticing that the new language isn't as fast as I'd like — some functions are lagging. So I want to find out why.

Right idea! You'll probably want to use a sampling profiler here. There's one called `cargo-flamegraph` that hooks into perf and generates flamegraphs, which help visualize where your program spends its time. It’s pretty straightforward:
